api: Track PMS / TrackHS API v1.9 (per-domain OpenAPI specs published at https://developer.trackhs.com/reference, covering PMS reservations/units/owners, CRM contacts/companies/leads, channel, guest-communications, and system domains; fetched 2026-07-03)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against Track PMS's complete published per-domain OpenAPI specs (hundreds of real operations across the PMS, CRM, channel, guest-communications, and system domains). Covered: the 4 legacy-parity read streams (reservations, guests, units, owners, at legacy's own paths/response shapes, unchanged) plus 2 new Pass B streams (contacts at the real /crm/contacts endpoint, unit_types) and 7 new write actions (create_reservation, create_unit, update_unit, create_owner, update_owner, create_contact, update_contact, delete_contact) covering every dialect-expressible core-resource mutation. Excluded: the vast per-reservation/per-unit/per-owner operational sub-resource graph (access codes, payment plans, fees, room configuration, housekeeping/channel mapping, owner statements/transactions/fractional-ownership, tax/policy configuration) as out_of_scope or requires_elevated_scope depending on whether it is admin taxonomy configuration or a narrow nested workflow; binary-payload endpoints (images, PDFs); and entire distinct CRM sub-domains (companies, leads, surveys, email/text marketing, campaigns, tags, inquiries) that would each need their own dedicated stream/write/schema set as a follow-on. See docs.md Known limits for two significant pre-existing legacy/real-API discrepancies this research surfaced (legacy's /reservations, /guests, /units, /owners paths and bare-array response shape do not match the currently-documented /pms/reservations, /crm/contacts /pms/units, /pms/owners paths and _embedded.<resource> HAL response envelope; and legacy's Bearer-token auth does not match the currently-documented per-tenant Basic/HMAC auth) — both are left as-is per the meta-rule (no accepted-input behavior change), not silently corrected.

excluded.reason: distinct CRM sub-domain (tasks, companies, leads, surveys, email-template/text/SMS marketing, inquiries, campaigns, tags) separate from the contacts resource this pass covers; each is its own object family with its own create/update/delete lifecycle and would need its own dedicated stream/write/schema set, a follow-on scope decision rather than an in-pass addition
